channel.title: Reverb — New Bass Guitars
channel.description: Recently listed bass guitars on Reverb

item.title: Fender Blacktop Precision Bass 2012 - 2014 - White Chrome Pearl — $700
item.description:
$700 · Excellent · Joe's Guitars

Barely used stunning Fender Blacktop bass with White Chrome Pearl finish. The original plastic is still mostly on the pickguard. Includes soft gig bag.

item.title: 2010 Ernie Ball Music Man Stingray Classic 4 Eldorado Gold — $2,100
item.description:
$2,100 · Very Good · Fanny’s House of Music

This rock-solid Stingray bass is a modern update on a true classic! It has a through-body bridge with a built-in mute kit (perfect for plunky Carol Kaye tones) and an active preamp with a 2-band EQ adjustment, making it particularly versatile for a single-pickup bass. It's an easy player and is in great condition!

Used/New/Vintage: Used

Repairs/Replacement Parts: Pickguard swapped (original in case), added Zero-Mod thumbrest

Neck Shape: C-shape

Cosmetic Issues: A blemish or two around the headstock, in excellent condition!

Wood: El Dorado Gold

Serial Number: B052621

Case: Original Hard Shell Case

Shipping costs vary by region. Items are returnable within 1 day of delivery. Items must be returned in original, as-shipped condition with all original packaging and no signs of use. Buyer assumes responsibility for all return shipping costs unless the item was not received as described.

item.title: Fender Aerodyne Jazz Bass MIJ JD-Serial 2015 Japanese Domestic Dolphin Grey — $1,538.91
item.description:
$1,538.91 · Excellent · GAS STATION GUITARS

Up for grabs is this scarce 2015 Dolphin Grey Fender Aerodyne Jazz Bass.These are great basses. Fender only made the DG for the Japanese domestic market so they don’t show up too much outside Japan. Most you will find outside of Japan will be black. This one is JD-Serial MIJ from 2015. Like all AJB’s it features a beautiful carved top & bound body. It has a DG matching headstock with cream body binding & black 3-ply pickguard (still has clear protective film covering it). All factory spec with a nice dense dark (almost black) stained rosewood fingerboard. It has had absolutely no breaks, repairs or mods. Neck is dead straight, plenty of life in the frets, truss rod fine. It is very clean no notable blemishes. Superb condition throughout. Complete with strong padded Fender branded softcase. Like all our basses this will come shipped in sturdy padded purpose designed bass box.

Gas Station Guitars is a UK Ltd company, and we proudly offer a no-questions 14-day refund policy on all instruments.

We have extensive experience shipping guitars safely worldwide, with professional packing methods developed to protect both hardcase and softcase instruments in transit. Every guitar is securely packaged using heavy-duty materials to ensure it arrives exactly as described.

At Gas Station Guitars, each instrument is carefully hand-selected. If a guitar doesn’t impress us in tone, feel, and overall character, it simply doesn’t make it into our inventory. If it’s listed by us, you can be confident it’s something special.

  • All photos shown are of the actual guitar
  • Every instrument is fully inspected and in hand—no drop-shipping
  • Shipped directly from us to you

Please note:

  • This guitar is not available for trades or part exchange
  • We are unable to offer holds or layaways

We ship WORLDWIDE including:

  • United Kingdom
  • European Union
  • United States
  • Canada
  • Australia

Professional service, accurate descriptions, and carefully curated instruments are at the core of everything we do.

Check out Gas Station Guitars on instagram 📸 (@gasstationguitars) & keep bang up to date with all our new and used inventory by visiting our website. We add new arrivals to the Gas Station Guitars website daily.

item.title: Rickenbacker 4003 2012 - Present - Midnight Blue — $3,500
item.description:
$3,500 · Excellent · Daniel's Gear Bazaar

Last year of production 2019 of Midnight Blue at the Rickenbacker Factory in Santa Ana California Mint condition, with no scratches or issues and plays like a dream. Comes with orginal case.

item.title: MTD Kingston Super 5 Electric Bass, 5-String (with Laurel Fingerboard), Black — $1,319
item.description:
$1,319 · Brand New · zZounds

The MTD Kingston Super 5-String Bass has powerful electronics, a premium finish, and a sleek carved body. These features all combine to make the Kingston Super a perfect blend of beauty, brawn, and tonal flexibility.

The Kingston Super delivers an unparalleled tonal range for any musical style. In addition to a 3-band active preamp, the Kingston Super sports a mini-switch for each MM-style pickup. While in the down position, the pickups are in hum-cancelling series mode, providing a bold punch and growl. In the middle position, the pickups are in single coil mode, making them sound bright and twangy. In the up position, the pickups are in hum-cancelling parallel mode, making them sound bright and sweet, with a slightly lower output than series mode. When both pickups are in single coil mode and the blend pot is centered, they are hum-cancelling. How's that, for some ear candy?

The V2 version is equipped with Hipshot open gear tuning machines and carbon fiber support rods, for increased stability and durability.

Features:

  • 5-String Bass
  • Ash Body with Flamed Maple Top
  • Dual MM style pickups
  • Maple Neck with Indian Laurel Fretboard
  • 3-band Active Preamp
  • Proprietary MTD Kingston electronics
  • Favored ergonomics
  • Buzz Feiten Tuning System

Specs: 

  • Electronics: Proprietary MTD Kingston
  • Preamp: 3 band active
  • Pickups: Dual MM style pickup set
  • Tuners: Hipshot open gear tuning machines
  • Body: Ash
  • Top: Flamed Maple
  • Neck Material: Maple
  • Fretboard: Indian Laurel
  • Scale Length: 35"
  • Number of Frets: 24
  • Neck Support: Carbon Fiber Truss rods in neck
  • Finish: Dr. Brown's Burst, Satin

item.title: Mayones Ori Enji 5Kitsune Orange — $2,799
item.description:
$2,799 · Very Good · Paiyapanichcnx

Very Good Condition
Truss rod and Bridge can adjustment
ORI logo was missing. I was contact to Mayones and then they send new one logo.
Back plate is sign of Mr.Dawid Dziewulski, CEO of Mayones Guitars
Include Gigbag

Body: Ash
Construction: Assymetrical bolt-on neck joint with a 5-screw mounted on ferrules Two additional graphite rods
Scale: V•Frets Bass Multiscale 34" - 37" (863 - 939 mm)
Neck:3-ply / WengeRadius:9.5" (241.3 mm)
Fingerboard: Wenge
Markers & Inlays:4mm white Perloid Double Offset Dots Original Luminlay™ side dots, SGM-23 Super Green in Black pipe, 3 mm
Neck dimensions: Width at nut – 46mm
Width XII fret – 63mm
Width XXIV fret – 72mm
Thickness I fret – 20mm
Thickness XII fret – 23mm
Truss rod: Double action truss rod
Frets: 24 Stainless Steel frets
Headstock: Multi-Angle Headstock
Nut: Ebony nut
Tuners layout: 3 + 2
Pickups / Electronics :H-H-H / Mayones Velvetrone® Grim VF Pickups
Mayones Musashi Nitōryū preamp (3 band EQ)
Control: 1 × Volume (Push-Pull Active - Passive), 1 × 6-way Rotary Switch, 1 × Bass/Treble, 1 × Middle
Headphone Amp
1/8" Stereo Jack Aux Input and Headphone out
Rechargeable Internal Power Supply /w backup 9v battery compartment
USB-C Charging Port w/ Dedicated Charger
Knobs: Custom Aluminium Knobs with Positioning Markers

item.title: Ibanez 2609B Black Eagle 2015 Reissue — $1,430.54
item.description:
$1,430.54 · Excellent · GMR's Gear Depot

Ibanez Black Eagle Reissue 2015 (Krist Novoselic)

MINT condition (no scratches, dings or any kind of damage)

Rare to find

Neck: Maple
Fingerboard: Bound Maple
Headstock: Graceful shaped headstock with F hole
Body: Attractive shaped Mahogany
Inlay: Intricate custom inlay
Pickguard: Decorated flying Black Eagle pickguard
Frets: Medium
Pickup: Super J pickups (Alnico magnetic)
Bridge: Std. bridge
Hardware color: Chrome

item.title: Fender Custom Shop LTD P-Bass Special JRN 2023 - Aged Ice Blue Metallic — $2,800
item.description:
$2,800 · Excellent · Rich's Voodoo Lounge of Tone

Killer P/J bass from the Custom Shop in a very cool finish - with matching headstock. I purchased new from SW. Case candy, COA, etc included. Weighs in at just under 10 lbs.

item.title: Peavey Millenium AC BXP 2010’s - Trans Purple — $490
item.description:
$490 · Excellent · Chicken Creek Ranch

Mint-condition Peavey Millennium AC BXP-5 active 5-string bass guitar with a beautiful wine-colored flame maple top and matching hard-shell case. This modern double-cutaway bass features active electronics, a comfortable maple neck, rosewood fretboard, and a basswood body with a figured maple top. It plays cleanly, looks excellent, and is well-suited for rock, metal, funk, worship, country, or any gig where a versatile active 5-string bass is needed.

item.title: Skervesen Bronto 6 2018 — $2,199
item.description:
$2,199 · Very Good · Paiyapanichcnx

Very Good Condition
Truss rod and Bridge can adjustment
All function work normally
Include gigbag
Got some ding and scratch
Have a little hum noise i try to fix but i found more many topic on talkbass forum it normally for Nordstrand Audio Big Single


Bronto 6 with poplar burl top, pau ferro middle layer, walnut body, 5-piece maple-wenge-bubinga neck, macassar ebony fretboard and Nordstrand Audio Big Single set!
Scale 34"-37"
Preamp : Darkglass Tone Capsule
Control : Master Volume, Neck Volume, Bridge Volume, Treble, Middle, Bass

item.title: Tune Technologies WBFM 5 1990 - Flame Maple — $2,500
item.description:
$2,500 · Excellent · Private Seller

great feel and sound

item.title: Squier Bronco Bass — $150
item.description:
$150 · Good · Cal's Stash

This Squier Bronco Bass has been such a good bass for me for 5 years. It’s in a good condition for its usage and is a great bass for people who want to get into playing. I’ve used this bass for performances and for recording, and it worked like a charm. Overall, a great bass for a low price.

item.title: Takamine GB72CE NAT Jumbo Body Style Cutaway Acoustic Electric Bass. Gloss Natural — $899
item.description:
$899 · Brand New · The Guitar Factory

With its mighty jumbo body style, solid-top construction, stunning cosmetic features and premium electronics, the GB72CE is a commanding acoustic-electric bass guitar that is built to perform. Featuring a solid spruce top and flame maple back and sides, the GB72CE produces all the volume and robust sound that players expect from a jumbo bass guitar. The slim mahogany neck and 16"-radius bound rosewood fingerboard provide great feel and playability, while the onboard Takamine TK-40B preamp system gives you a built-in tuner, three-band EQ and gain controls, mid-shift control, bass-boost switch and EQ bypass switch for the ultimate in versatility and sound quality. Other great features include a dovetail neck joint, synthetic bone nut and bridge saddle, rosewood headcap, abalone rosette, pearl dot fingerboard inlays, gold die-cast tuners, and a beautiful gloss finish.

Body Shape Jumbo Bass Cutaway
Top Solid Spruce
Back Flame Maple
Sides Flame Maple
Neck Mahogany
Fingerboard Rosewood
Finish Gloss Natural
Electronics TK-40B
Nut Width 45mm
Scale Length 864mm
Length 1214.7mm
Width 435mm
Depth At End 125mm
Case
GC-B
Case Included No

item.title: Takamine GB30CE NAT Venetian Style Cutaway Acoustic Electric Bass. Gloss Natural — $799
item.description:
$799 · Brand New · The Guitar Factory

The GB30CE is a stage-worthy acoustic/electric bass guitar that features solid-top construction, a soft Venetian-style cutaway and a superior-sounding Takamine electronics system. Ready for any performance situation, the GB30CE features a solid spruce top and mahogany back and sides that produce a rich, resonant sound. The slim mahogany neck and 16"-radius rosewood fingerboard provide great feel and playability, while the onboard Takamine TK-40B preamp system gives you a built-in tuner, three-band EQ and gain controls, mid-shift control, bass-boost switch and EQ bypass switch for the ultimate in versatility and sound quality. Other great features include a dovetail neck joint, synthetic bone nut and bridge saddle, rosewood headcap, pearl dot fingerboard inlays, abalone rosette, chrome die-cast tuners and a beautiful gloss finish.

Body Shape Jumbo Cutaway
Top Solid Spruce
Back Mahogany
Sides Mahogany
Neck Mahogany
Fingerboard Rosewood
Finish Gloss Natural
Electronics TK-40B
Nut Width 45mm
Scale Length 864mm
Length 1214.7mm
Width 435mm
Depth At End 125mm
Case
GC-B
Case Included No

item.title: Takamine GB30CE BLK Venetian Style Cutaway Acoustic Electric Bass. Gloss Black — $799
item.description:
$799 · Brand New · The Guitar Factory

The GB30CE is a stage-worthy acoustic/electric bass guitar that features solid-top construction, a soft Venetian-style cutaway and a superior-sounding Takamine electronics system.

Ready for any performance situation, the GB30CE features a solid spruce top and mahogany back and sides that produce a rich, resonant sound. The slim mahogany neck and 16”-radius laurel fingerboard provide great feel and playability, while the onboard Takamine TK-40B preamp system gives you a built-in tuner, three-band EQ and gain controls, mid-shift control, bass-boost switch and EQ bypass switch for the ultimate in versatility and sound quality.

Other great features include a dovetail neck joint, synthetic bone nut and bridge saddle, laurel headcap, pearl dot fingerboard inlays, abalone rosette, chrome die-cast tuners and a beautiful gloss finish. Available in Natural and Black.

Top Solid Spruce
Back Mahogany
Sides Mahogany
Neck Mahogany
Finger Board Laurel
Nut Width 1.77" (45 mm)
Electronics TK-40B
Finish Black

item.title: Fodera Monarch 4 Standard Special 2025 - Natural Myrtle Satin — $6,250
item.description:
$6,250 · Excellent · Chris's Gear Garage

Fodera Monarch 4 Standard Special Electric Bass Guitar - Natural Myrtle Satin with Black Hardware and Ebony Fretboard,

Sweetwater Exclusive

Semi-hollowbody Electric Bass with Myrtle Top, Alder Body, Maple Neck, Ebony Fingerboard, and 2 Dual-coil Pickups - Natural Myrtle Satin

REMOVABLE RAMP

Like New-Played Very Little-Only In Studio-Smoke Free Environment

Plays Amazing-Sounds Amazing

SER# M4SS133

8.8 LBs

The Fodera Monarch 4 Standard Special — Only at Sweetwater!

The Fodera Monarch 4 Standard Special is designed from the ground up for optimized performance. For supreme comfort, this unique bass guitar features a lightweight, sleekly sculpted semi-hollow body with an eye-popping natural myrtle top and a super-fast 3-piece hard-rock maple neck capped with a smooth-as-glass ebony fingerboard. Top it off with the legendary combination of Fodera Dual Coil pickups and a Fodera x Trickfish preamp, and you have one of the finest basses you’ll ever have the privilege to play. This gorgeous, heirloom-quality boutique instrument is one of a kind — and it’s only available at Sweetwater. So don’t miss out — grab the Fodera Monarch 4 Standard Special while you can!

Exceptional tonewoods, appointments, and playability

Premium tonewoods meet professional performance in this Fodera Monarch 4 Standard Special bass. The hand-selected semi-hollow alder body, highly figured myrtle top, and 3-piece hard-rock maple neck serve up a classic combination of resonance and sustain, while the silky-smooth ebony fingerboard and Plek’d frets deliver the fluidity you need to nail everything from rootsy grooves to demanding technical passages at ludicrous speed. Add to that the powerful connection you get from a fully adjustable high-mass bell-brass bridge, and the Monarch 4 Standard Special delivers the look and feel you’ll only get from a premium, luthier-built instrument.

Fodera pickups deliver fat, punchy tone without the hum

The Monarch 4 Standard Special packs a pair of Fodera Dual Coil pickups designed in a collaboration between Fodera and Seymour Duncan. They’re built with passive dual coils, which integrate with the natural qualities of the tonewoods to produce added punch on the low end, focused mids, and smooth highs. Fodera Dual Coil pickups deliver a beautifully warm and organic tone with outstanding note definition; while the coils are wired in parallel for noise-free performance, you’d swear the Monarch Standard Special was loaded with single-coils. They sound great whether you use them in passive mode or with the onboard 3-band preamp.

Craft your signature voice with the Fodera x Trickfish preamp

The Fodera x Trickfish preamp is an enhanced iteration of Fodera’s standard preamp, developed in collaboration with the renowned tone experts at Trickfish Amplification. This exceptional preamp offers unrivaled performance, featuring a lower noise floor and extended battery life compared to previous models. It’s compatible with both active and passive pickups, and it operates in both Active and Passive modes. Additionally, it features a passive Tone knob that functions in both modes, along with a shapeable active EQ. The Fodera x Trickfish preamp also allows for deep configuration via internal dip switches, enabling you to easily adjust the preamp’s overall output gain, the frequency sweep of its passive Tone control, its lowpass and highpass filters, and the frequency center of its active Midrange tone control — no soldering or additional circuit jumpers required. With the Fodera x Trickfish preamp, dialing in your signature sound is moments away.

Fodera: superior playability, sonics, and build quality

Established in 1983 by custom luthier Vinny Fodera and pro bassist Joey Lauricella, Fodera enjoys a reputation as the Ferrari of boutique basses. Fodera basses give you the superior playability, sonics, and build quality of the custom models played by bass legends such as Victor Wooten, Anthony Jackson, and Oteil Burbridge — in a more affordable axe. Handcrafted in the US with the same materials by the same top luthiers, Fodera “production” instruments offer no options, allowing them to be built in small runs instead of one-offs, thus holding down the cost. Play a Fodera and take a step up to boutique quality. You’ve arrived.

Tech Specs

  • Number of Strings:4
  • Left-/Right-handed:Right-handed
  • Body Shape:Monarch 4
  • Body Material:Alder (body), Figured Myrtle (top)
  • Color:Natural
  • Body Finish:Satin
  • Neck Material:3-piece Hard Rock Maple
  • Neck Shape:Fodera Standard
  • Neck Joint:Bolt-on
  • Radius:12"-20" Compound
  • Fingerboard Material:Ebony
  • Number of Frets:24, Large, Nickel
  • Scale Length:34"
  • Scale Length Type:Standard-scale
  • Nut Material:Brass
  • Bridge/Tailpiece:Fodera Standard
  • Tuners:Gotoh
  • Neck Pickup:Fodera/Duncan Dual Coils 70's
  • Bridge Pickup:Fodera/Duncan Dual Coils 70's
  • Controls:1 x Volume, 1 x Tone, 1 x 2-band EQ
  • Case/Gig Bag: New Style TSA Hardshell Case
  • Manufacturer Part Number:M4SS-Alder-Myrtle-Ebony-BLK

item.title: Cordoba Mini Bass MH-E Around 2020 - Natural — $230
item.description:
$230 · Very Good · Richard's Gear Depot

The Cordoba Mini II Bass is an acoustic electric bass with a smaller body size, fingerboard width, and scale length that's perfect for younger or petite players and also electric guitarists seeking an affordable, accessible — and fun — bass instrument. Its smaller dimensions also make the Mini II ideal for air travel and weekend getaways. But make no mistake: the Cordoba Mini II is a legit acoustic electric bass that just happens to be small!

Features:

  • Mahogany back and sides
  • Fan-braced mahogany top
  • Satin polyurethane finish
  • Mahogany neck, C profile, satin polyurethane finish
  • Composite fingerboard, 19 frets, dot inlays
  • Composite bridge; bone saddle
  • Bone nut; Cordoba tuners
  • Onboard electronics with tuner

Unlike other small basses that have gummy strings, the Cordoba Mini II has regular steel strings. A Gator soft gig bag included.

item.title: Fender Jazz Bass 1978 - Natural - Left Handed — $3,638.99
item.description:
$3,638.99 · Very Good · Giacomo's Shop

Basso Fender Jazz del 1978

Colore Natural

Per mancini

Tutto originale, presenta segni di usura sulla paletta. Copri ponte originale Fender presente.

Vendo con custodia (no Fender), cinghia e cavo che si vedono in foto.

Preferisco ritiro a mano.

item.title: Squier Contemporary Active Jazz Bass V HH 2018 - Present - Black — $446.33
item.description:
$446.33 · Good · My Music Shop

I have a Squier contemporary active jazz bass V HH for sale. it will come with a hard case and packed well for shipping. It has been gigged quite extensively but there are no big damage on the bass. I’ve gotten a black pickguard as well as a custom guard with inlay for the portion that’s around the knobs. it is a beautiful bass. Sounds great too.

item.title: Ibanez SR1205E-VNF Premium Soundgear 2011 - Vintage Natural Flat — $1,229.72
item.description:
$1,229.72 · Excellent · Mr_Goose

Only used briefly a handful of times, remained in case and in perfect working order with new battery. Ibanez premium case, manual and Ibanez branded multitool included. There are slight scratches and dents around the sides of the guitar which I have provided photos of.


What a beauty... and tonal monster. The Ibanez SR1205E proves that striking looks and timeless bass tone don't have to cost a fortune. Indeed, the SR1205E is an outstanding value. Start with that mahogany body, topped with figured Ovangkol. Great looks. Then add in the smooth-playing 5-piece Wenge/Bubinga neck, decked out with a rosewood fingerboard and abalone oval inlays. This is one great-looking, great-playing bass. Still, the SR1205E's first priority is tone, and that's just what the two Nordstrand Big Single pickups deliver. And with the 3-band EQ, you'll have more than enough tonal and shaping power to hit the stage or studio with any band. Simply put, the Ibanez SR1205E is a great bass.


Ibanez SR1205E Features at a Glance:

  • Vintage Natural Flat Finish
  • Mahogany Body
  • Figured Ovangkol Top
  • 5-pc Wenge/Bubinga SR5 Neck with KTS titanium rods
  • Medium Frets
  • Rosewood Fretboard
  • Abalone Oval Inlay
  • Mono-Rail IV Bridge
  • Neck Pickup: Nordstrand Big Single
  • Bridge Pickup: Nordstrand Big Single
  • EQB-IIISC 3-Band EQ w/EQ Bypass Switch
  • Gig Bag Included

item.title: Warwick Thumb BO 5 2003 - Natural Oil — $2,207.26
item.description:
$2,207.26 · Very Good · Ryan's Roman Gear

All original except for the tuning machines. Everything is clean, strait and functions as it should. Includes a fender rigid soft case.

item.title: 1990s BC Rich Warlock Bass - Lime Green — $899.99
item.description:
$899.99 · Fair · Bazaar Guitar

Circa 1990s

BC Rich

Platinum Series

Warlock Bass

Lime Green

This Bass is in used vintage condition.

There are many signs of age and use. There are chips to the paint which have been touched up. There are chips to the lower horns.

The neck is straight. The frets show average wear for its age. The action is low. The strings are new.

The electronics are working with minimal scratch to the pots.

There is no case or bag included.

***This item is described to the best of our ability. There is always the possibility that a detail was overlooked. if you are uncomfortable buying a guitar / parts / pedals online please pass this by. 

If you have any questions ask them BEFORE purchasing. RETURNS ARE NOT ACCEPTED / THERE ARE NO PARTIAL REFUNDS UNDER ANY CIRCUMSTANCE.

___________
The Fine Print

This used / vintage guitar / audio gear is sold in As-Described condition so please ask all questions before purchasing . I will be happy to send additional photos. 

Payment is through reverb and shipping is done with Reverb safe shipping insurance so everyone is protected. 

(Tracked and Insured with Reverb Safe Shipping) 

In the event of damage to the item refunds will be handled by Reverb and safe shipping.


Additional Information: 

***Payment is required within 24 hours. If payment is not completed the order will be cancelled and the item relisted. 

* Guitars will be shipped de-tuned, they are not playable out of the box. The shipping process may expose guitars to different temperatures and humidity levels. The guitar should be fully setup to your preference once or arrives.

* I do not offer partial refunds for any reason. Given the abuse of return policies and parts being removed or items being used and returned there are NO RETURNS ACCEPTED. IF THE ITEM IS DAMAGED IN SHIPPING the claim will be handled by Reverb Safe Shipping. 

*In the event of repairable shipping damage the buyer agrees to have the item repaired. If the buyer refuses the repair and insists on a return and refund there will be a 50% return and repair fee along with all shipping costs withheld from the refund. 

If you simply changed your mind after purchase and item has been shipped there is a 50% restocking fee. 

* Pedals are tested before being sold and as described in the listing. The buyer should understand signal chain and pedal order. Pedals will interact differently in various combinations. 

*If a pedal, part or guitar is returned and tests good there is a 50% restocking and all shipping costs will be deducted from your refund.

item.title: Epiphone EB-0 2009 - Ebony - Gator Gig Bag - Small Neck — $225
item.description:
$225 · Excellent · Artie Fufkin Bazaar

This bass plays well and has little to no wear. Clean neck pockets. Fresh looking frets. See photos.

Neck is small and perfect for smaller hands.

Comes with nice Gator gig bag.

item.title: Fender 2004 Mike Dirnt Precision Bass Hybrid W/ Squier 2009 Mike Dirnt Rosewood Neck — $595
item.description:
$595 · Good · Branson’s Basses

Here’s a very cool Mike Dirnt Precision Bass Hybrid that was assembled from a 2004 Fender Dirnt body refinished Natural, and a 2009 Squier Dirnt neck with Rosewoid fretboard.

Bought this refin Fender Mike Dirnt bass with its original neck but soon found out that it was among many other 2004 Fender Dirnt basses that had bad truss rods.

After exploring the costs and hassle of removing/replacing the rod, and/or steaming the original neck I came up with this hybrid Fender & Squier Dirnt Bass idea, and honestly it works great on this body, no shims, fits nice and tight, plays super well. This bass is now a better bass, period, don’t let a headstock decal put you off.

The Natural refinish job is about standard for a home job, a couple swirls etc but is a quite handsome bass overall with beautiful grain. Fairly sure this body is Ash, but I’m no expert. The fretboard on the neck is nice and dark, frets in great shape. Rear of the neck is nice. Tuners work well and are stock.

Comes with a gig bag, no other parts included.

item.title: Squier Vintage Modified Jaguar Bass Special SS 2012 - 2018 - Candy Apple Red — $449
item.description:
$449 · Very Good · Drew's Gear Garage

Squire mod Jaguar bass. 2017 crafted in Indonesia model. This one is in very good condition, does show some light scuffs on pickguard play area, very light surface marks on headstock and rear of body, hard to capture. Just cleaned, polished and fret board conditioned, plays very nice, ready to go! Does include DiMarzio straplock strap. Will ship expertly packed.

item.title: Sterling SUB StingRay Ray4HH 2024 - Present - Chopper Blue — $550
item.description:
$550 · Excellent · J's Gear Outlet

Love how this bass plays, I just never get a chance to use it. Strung with flat wounds so it feels great. Comes with Levy’s padded bass strap ($30) and Fender FB620 Gig Bag ($50).

item.title: 2016 Ibanez TMB30-IV Standard Talman Bass , Ivory — $189
item.description:
$189 · Excellent · Dimma's Guitar Sanctuary

From personal collection. Great condition. Plays well.

Ships fast CONUS states Only


Model Info below:

The TMB30 is a Talman Bass series solid body, short scale, electric bass model introduced by Ibanez for 2016. It is produced in Indonesia as part of the standard line.

The TMB30 features an asymmetric double-cutaway mahogany body bolted to a maple neck with 30" (762mm) scale a 20-fret rosewood fingerboard with white dot position markers. Components include a pair of Ibanez Dynamix passive pickups mounted in a black pickguard with a Precision pickup at the neck and a Jazz pickup at the bridge, a B10 bridge with 19mm string spacing, a plastic nut, and Ibanez open tuning machines.

item.title: Epiphone Newport One owner since mid 60's - No so good. Used by my dad for many years — $1,500
item.description:
$1,500 · Fair · Ron

Still sounds great. My dad used it in many bands for many years.

item.title: 1970’s Unbranded Shortscale Tele Bass 30.5” Scale + Vintage Case MIJ Japan Ibanez 2353 / Aria T Style — $899
item.description:
$899 · Good · Firstone

1970’s Unbranded Shortscale Tele Bass 30.5” Scale + Vintage Case MIJ Japan Ibanez 2353 / Aria T Style



Super cool. All electronics work as intended. No name on headstock but shares many similarities with Ibanez and Aria basses from this era. Bass has plenty of dings, dents, paint chips, finish cracks etc but still a fun playing bass.

Bass has black tape wound strings.

Comes with what may be the original case although this case looks like it could fit a full scale bass. All latches and hinges work and handle is secure. Case has some wear as shown. The handle is brown where rest of case is black so maybe it is a replacement.



Bass plays fine as is but any personal cleaning, setups, maintenance or repairs will be the new owners responsibility.

This is a pre-owned bass that does show wear commensurate of age and use.

See pics and video for more details. 

International buyers are responsible for any taxes, duties, fees etc. I may be unable to ship to some countries based on size restrictions or other factors.

item.title: Fernandes Jazz bass 1981 - Sunburst — $1,730.01
item.description:
$1,730.01 · Excellent · François Haenel

Parce qu'elle fait double emploi, je mets à la vente cette splendide Fernandes made in Japan de 1981. Tout est d'origine, l'action est basse, touche non usée, trussrod parfaitement fonctionnel. Quelques marques d'usage liées à son âge (44 ans tout de même), mais c'est bien plus beau qu'un relicage mal fait !. Corps en Aulne (sustain énorme pas besoin de chevalet highmass, on a bien le "mwaaa" a la Jaco !) , manche érable et touche palissandre, la belle sera dans un fly pour la protéger. Envoi en colissimo super bien emballé pour 50 € supplémentaires.

L'occasion de posséder une fretless d'une qualité égale (voire supérieure) à certaines Fender hors de prix sans vendre un rein. Cette basse est un investissement, pas une dépense : Elle prendra de la valeur avec le temps si vous en prenez soin.

Cordes filets ronds neuves, touche absolument pas usée.

EN CADEAU UNE JOLIE BASSE D'ENTRÉE DE GAMME JAMES SPIRIT :elle fait le job. (port de 30€ néanmoins à votre charge)

item.title: Gibson EB-3 1971 — $3,221.40
item.description:
$3,221.40 · Very Good · François Haenel

Je mets à la vente cette Gibson Eb3 2e Gen (avec le micro manche légèrement reculé par rapport aux 1eres. Les potentiomètres datent de 1970,la basse qui porte le numéro 108392 certainement de 1971. Elle est dans un état remarquable pour son âge mais j'ai du la remettre à l'état d'origine. Effectivement, comme beau coup d'instruments à l'époque, des musiciens avaient envie de rajouter un micro ce qui a été le cas entre les 2 micros existant. J'ai donc placé un cache noir pour masquer la cavité et comme il y avait 3 interrupteurs sur le pickguard, j'ai collé une plaque adhésive retaillée. Pour lesnouristes, un pickguard d'origine coûte dans les 100€. Tout fonctionne à merveille, le son est gras, le varitone permet pas mal de combinaisons de tonalités différentes. Le trussrod fonctionnel, manche bien réglé, c'est un régal de jouer sur la même basse (shortscale) que Jack Bruce et Bill Wyman!

I'm selling this Gibson EB3 2nd Gen (with the neck pickup slightly set back compared to the earlier models). The potentiometers date from 1970, but the bass, serial number 108392, is certainly from 1971. It's in remarkable condition for its age, but I had to restore it to its original state. Indeed, like many instruments at the time, some musicians wanted to add a pickup, which is what happened between the two existing ones. So I put a black cover over the cavity, and since there were three switches on the pickguard, I glued on a cut-out adhesive plate. For those interested, an original pickguard costs around €100. Everything works perfectly, the sound is fat, and the Varitone allows for a good number of different tone combinations. The truss rod is functional, the neck is well set up, and it's a real treat to play the same bass (shortscale) as Jack Bruce. and Bill Wyman!

item.title: Dingwall Combustion 5 — $3,460.02
item.description:
$3,460.02 · Excellent · MarkoParts

Dingwall Super Fatty II canadian pickups paid 660€

Mike Pope flexcore 4 band preamp paid 350€

Gotoh Gb350 tuners, overall 50g lighter than licened hipshots

Puretone Jack

Black hardware with black Dunlop straplocks and a flushmout straplock on the back just like canadian models

6 way pickup selector, all parallel modes, B-BM-BN-BMN-MN-N, because all modes are parallel there is no usual pop when changing pickups

Nova Dingwall bridge


4,26kg almost perfectly balanced


No shipping outside the EU

item.title: Fodera Yin yang 2025 - Satin — $4,500
item.description:
$4,500 · Excellent · Michael 's Gear Outlet

brand spanking new- never left the house with it. Bought on impulse and changed my mind. Has been hanging on my wall collecting dust.

Comes with COA, original Fedora case. Mint condition, not a mark on it.

item.title: Charvel EOB-50B Made in Japan MiJ Jazz Bass - Blue/Purple — $690.81
item.description:
$690.81 · Good · Michał's Gear Outlet

For sale: a bass you won't find in any Western catalogue - the Charvel EOV-50B, a Japanese Jazz Bass from the second half of the 90s, built at the Chushin Gakki factory. It also comes in a rarely seen, very striking finish - metallic blue shifting into violet, with a fine sparkle in the light.

Charvel is mostly associated with glam-era superstrats, but the brand had a second, lesser-known life. Japanese Charvels had been built at Chushin Gakki since 1986 - the factory behind the legendary Model Series and the top MIJ Jacksons. In the mid-90s Chushin took over the brand on the Japanese market and produced models strictly for domestic use (JDM). The EOV-50 is one of them: a jazz bass that never officially left Japan - you could count the European examples on one hand.

It features a classic JJ layout with 60s-style pickup spacing, passive vol/vol/tone electronics, and a maple neck with maple fingerboard and black dots. Styling-wise it's an unusual jazz bass: a non-standard headstock, electronics mounted on a metal plate, and uniform satin-silver hardware tying it all together. The bridge is seriously solid, weighing a good twice as much as a standard bent-plate unit. Tonally the bass is fairly bright, but with a surprising amount of midrange and a slightly nasal character; it sounds great with the tone rolled back.

The neck is thin, slim and satin-finished, with just 39 mm at the nut - comfortable also for smaller hands. Weight: 4.3 kg.

Cosmetically I'd rate the condition as average-plus, technically as very good. The sides and back have plenty of dings and scratches, but the front looks decent, and the pickguard and headstock are flawless. The satin hardware has survived very well and works as it should. The frets are tall and chunky with years of life left, the truss rod is fully functional, and the action is set below 2 mm at the 12th fret. All electronics and hardware are original.

This is a private sale. The bass can be tried out in Poznań, Poland; safe, well-packed shipping from Poland is no problem.

item.title: Ernie Ball Music Man StingRay 5 2004, Lava Pearl, Full Restoration, Nordstrand Pickup, Pro Set Up! — $2,599.99
item.description:
$2,599.99 · Excellent · The Stray Chord Instrument Rescue Centre

🔥 Sunday Special 🔥


This is not your ordinary Stray Of The Day.


🦁 Meet Leo 🦁


🏷️ Adoption Fee: £1850 🏷️


Ernie Ball Music Man StingRay 5, 2004, Lava Pearl


San Luis Obispo, California, USA


Ready for rehoming, this incredible USA-made Music Man StingRay 5 is finished in the rare and long-discontinued Lava Pearl.


Under normal light, Leo wears a deep burnt-copper finish. Move him beneath the lights and that three-stage pearl paint erupts into fiery reds, warm bronze and glowing orange flares. It is one of those finishes photographs simply cannot capture properly. He changes every time you look at him.


Leo has also been one of the most intensive and rewarding restorations to come through our workshop so far.


After spending almost two decades cased inside a damp storage locker, he arrived with a whole host of problems. His neck had developed a severe bow and twist, the electrics were partially seized and full of crackles, his clarity had disappeared, the G string suffered from persistent fret buzz, and areas of exposed timber had developed mould.


Cosmetically, though, he had somehow survived remarkably well. Beneath the effects of storage sat an exceptional 2004 StingRay 5, built at the Music Man factory in San Luis Obispo, California, with one of the most recognisable bass designs and sounds ever created.


Leo Fender’s bones were still there.


He just needed saving.


Jordan began with a full sanitisation and decontamination of the instrument. Every affected surface, cavity and exposed section of timber was carefully cleaned and treated, removing the contamination caused by years of damp storage before any mechanical work began.


Sarah then took charge of the finish. Leo’s individually selected solid ash body had survived with only one notable chip, measuring approximately 2mm by 3mm. This was carefully stabilised with clear CA to prevent any further damage while retaining the honest history of the instrument. The body was then painstakingly hand-buffed to remove years of haze and restore the depth of that incredible Lava Pearl finish.


Finally, it received a protective carnauba wax polish, helping to guard the lacquer against moisture and salts while giving Leo a deep, mirror-like shine.


The back of the neck was carefully cleaned and restored without burying its natural feel beneath heavy lacquer or polish. That familiar Music Man gunstock oil and wax character has been preserved, leaving the neck smooth, fast and wonderfully natural beneath the hand.


Then came the difficult part.


The one-piece maple neck had developed both a pronounced bow and a twist during its years in storage. Jordan began with a carefully controlled decompression and heat realignment process, gradually bringing the neck back into shape without forcing or shocking the timber.


Once stable, the neck pocket and six-bolt joint were precisely reset and squared, restoring the correct relationship between the neck and solid ash body. This brought the neck back into alignment, improved the playing geometry and secured the strong transfer of resonance and sustain that these instruments are known for.


The fretwork had also suffered from the changing moisture levels. Several frets had lifted or sprouted as the timber moved, so Jordan reseated and stabilised them before carrying out a complete fret level, crown and polish.


Every fret was brought back into line, carefully reshaped and polished to a smooth mirror finish. The fret ends were dressed, the playing surface was checked across the full neck, and that persistent G-string buzz was finally eliminated.


This is the level of attention an American StingRay deserves. They are iconic for a reason.


The rosewood fingerboard then underwent a multistage clean and conditioning process. After years of neglect, damp storage and the intensive neck treatment required to save it, the board needed far more than a quick wipe with oil. It has been carefully restored to bring back its dark colour, smooth playing feel and natural lustre.


Under the hood, the restoration continued.


The electrical cavity was thoroughly cleaned, and the controls and switches were carefully flushed and treated to remove the contamination left by years in damp storage. The seized movement, crackles and loss of clarity have gone. The controls now move freely, operate cleanly and allow the bass to deliver the powerful, articulate response it was built for.


At some point during his life, Leo’s original owner upgraded him with a boutique Nordstrand pickup. With its oversized exposed pole pieces and muscular Alnico character, it gives this StingRay an enormous voice.


Played clean, it can be crisp, articulate and beautifully controlled for jazz, funk and studio work. Add some drive and it delivers a huge midrange bark with the punch, attack and authority that make a StingRay so instantly recognisable.


The three-way selector unlocks three distinct voices:


Series mode delivers the highest output, with a thick, aggressive bark that is ideal for heavy rock and driven tones.


Single-coil mode gives a cleaner, more open response with scooped, articulate character.


Parallel mode delivers that classic 1970s StingRay sound, with the snap, punch and percussive attack that made these basses legendary.


The active 9-volt preamp gives complete control through master volume, bass, middle and treble boost and cut. Combined with the instrument’s factory shielding and hum-cancelling design, Leo remains impressively quiet while offering a genuinely enormous tonal range.


To complete the restoration, Jordan carried out a full professional setup and fitted Leo with a fresh set of Ernie Ball Regular Slinky strings.


The result is a bass that feels alive again.


Fast beneath the hand. Powerful through an amplifier. Clear, responsive and capable of moving from warm, controlled studio tones to huge, aggressive rock sounds without ever losing definition.


Leo has not been disguised as an untouched collector’s piece. His restoration and Nordstrand pickup upgrade form part of his history and will be fully disclosed. What he offers is something far more interesting: a genuine American-made StingRay 5 that was brought back from the edge through careful, extensive workshop restoration.


He still carries the class and retro character of an early-2000s Music Man, but now has the reliability, playability and dynamic range needed for modern studio, stage and touring use.


Whether he is heading into a professional recording session, climbing aboard a tour bus, holding down a heavy rhythm section or simply becoming the finest sofa bass you have ever owned, Leo is ready for his next chapter.


USA made.

Rare Lava Pearl finish.

Boutique Nordstrand pickup.

Professionally restored.

Five strings and one enormous voice.


Leo was built to be played.


We made sure he could be again.

item.title: Hamer Slammer short scale double cut Bass — $299
item.description:
$299 · Good · Kennys

Amazing woodgrain. Amazing short scale bass. Plays wonderful. Great bass. Passive electronics. New strings.

item.title: Warwick RockBass Streamer Standard, 5-String - Honey Violin Transparent Satin Honey Violin Transparent — $912.73
item.description:
$912.73 · Excellent · Alen's Shop

Brand new bass guitar, professionally set up with Ernie Ball strings

new improved 2024 model

low action

includes RockBass gig bag


Bass guitar specifications (manufacturer's page): 849eur

Warwick RockBass Streamer Standard, 5-String - Honey Violin Transparent Satin

  • Warwick RockBass Streamer Standard
  • 5-string electric bass
  • 34" long scale
  • 2-piece solid Carolina Pine body
  • 4-piece laminated Maple neck with black veneer stripes
  • two carbon bars for neck reinforcement, from 12th to 24th fret
  • two-way steel trussrod
  • bolt-on construction
  • Wenge fingerboard
  • 26" radius
  • 24 jumbo titanium-brass alloy frets (width: 2.9 mm / height: 1.4 mm)
  • Ball end frets with IFT (Invisible Fret Technology)
  • Just-A-Nut III Tedur nut
  • nut width 45.0 mm / 1.8"
  • glow-in-the-dark side dots
  • passive MEC Vintage Humbucker pickups
  • active 2-band electronics
  • controls for Volume / Balance / Treble / Bass
  • copper shielded electronic compartment, pickup routings with shielding paint
  • Warwick Easy-Access electronics compartment and truss rod cover
  • threaded inserts for pickup screws
  • Warwick machine heads
  • 2-piece Warwick 3D bridge
  • string spacing 16.5 mm / 21/32"
  • distance B to G string 66 mm / 2 19/32"
  • Warwick S-Security Locks
  • Warwick Red Strings Bass String Set, Stainless Steel, .045-.135, Medium Scale (42301 M 5B 045/135")
  • Honey Violin Transparent Satin finish
  • chrome hardware
  • incl. Warwick Strap und Warwick Fret Protector
  • incl. Warwick trussrod tool, hex key 1,5 mm, Warwick microfiber cloth and Warwick S-Security Locks and 9 V battery
  • incl. RockBass Starline Premium Gig Bag
  • approx. weight 4.0 kg / 8.8 lbs.

item.title: Schecter Stargazer-5 Electric Bass Gloss Black — $1,099
item.description:
$1,099 · Brand New · LV Guitars

Schecter Stargazer-5 Electric Bass Gloss Black

The Stargazer-5 delivers a comfortable, modern playing experience with its thin "C" neck profile that responds naturally to fast passages and intricate fretwork. Extended low-end range from the five-string configuration combines with articulate midrange tone that cuts through any mix, striking a balance between warmth and definition suitable for jazz fusion, progressive rock, and modern metal applications. The gloss black finish complements the instrument's refined character and clear, resonant voice across both aggressive picking and nuanced finger-style playing.

Playability & Build

The 35" scale with 22 jumbo frets and a 16" fretboard radius creates a responsive platform for both fast passages and smooth bends. A 3-piece maple neck with consistent dimensions—0.787" at the 1st fret and 0.866" at the 12th—delivers stability and comfortable ergonomics, while the Graph Tech XL Black Tusq 45mm nut ensures reliable tuning and clean string-to-fret contact. Deep Insert Joint with Ultra Access construction provides excellent upper-fret access on this five-string design.

Tone & Electronics

A Schecter USA M-25 5-string bridge pickup and Schecter USA J-25 5-string neck pickup deliver articulate, defined tones across the frequency spectrum. Dual mini-toggle switches provide series/split/parallel voicing options on the bridge pickup and coil-tap functionality on the neck pickup, allowing players to shape everything from tight, punchy lows to warm, singing highs without leaving the instrument.

Key Features
  • Body Material: Mahogany with gloss black finish and white 1-ply binding
  • Neck: 3-piece maple with thin "C" profile
  • Fretboard: Maple with 22 jumbo frets and block inlays
  • Scale: 35 inches
  • Fretboard Radius: 16 inches
  • Nut: Graph Tech XL Black Tusq, 45mm width
  • Bridge Pickup: Schecter USA M-25 5-string with series/split/parallel switching
  • Neck Pickup: Schecter USA J-25 5-string with coil-tap option
  • Hardware: TonePros M Series tuners and Diamond Custom Bass bridge with chrome finish
  • Controls: Volume, Blend, Tone with dual mini-toggle switches
  • Total Length: Approximately 45.5 inches
  • Side Dot Markers: Dots for easy fret reference

This Stargazer-5 combines premium component selection with versatile pickup switching to provide a professional-grade instrument ready for studio work, live performance, or extended creative exploration.

item.title: Schecter Reaper-5 Lefty Electric Bass Natural Satin — $1,549
item.description:
$1,549 · Brand New · LV Guitars

Schecter Reaper-5 Lefty Electric Bass Natural Satin

This left-handed Schecter Reaper-5 delivers a supremely comfortable playing experience with its contoured body and buttery smooth satin finish, making extended sessions feel effortless. The thin "C" maple neck with walnut stringers and carbon fiber reinforcement carves through your hand with precision, while the 35" scale and 24 jumbo frets provide the reach and speed demanded by modern bass lines. The punchy, articulate tone cuts through any mix with clarity, delivering tight low-end punch and snappy mids that respond beautifully across genres from metal and progressive rock to funk and jazz fusion.

Playability & Feel

The thin "C" neck profile with maple and walnut construction reinforced by 2 carbon fiber rods provides both speed and stability, while the 35" scale length extends your range without sacrificing comfort. The ebony fingerboard with 24 jumbo frets and 16" radius enables precise articulation across the entire neck, and the Graph Tech XL Black Tusq nut (45mm) ensures consistent string response from the headstock forward.

Tone & Electronics

The dual Schecter USA M-50 and P-50 pickups paired with the Darkglass Tone Capsule 18v EQ system provide comprehensive tonal shaping via master volume, blend control, and dedicated bass/mid/upper-mid contour knobs. This active electronics setup delivers articulate, punchy response with exceptional clarity and the ability to dial in everything from aggressive attack to warm, rounded lows.

Key Features
  • Left-handed configuration with swamp ash body and figured poplar burl top
  • Natural Satin finish
  • Bolt-on construction
  • Maple neck with walnut stringers and 2 carbon fiber reinforcement rods
  • Thin "C" neck shape with 35" scale length
  • Ebony fingerboard with 24 jumbo frets and 16" radius
  • Pearloid offset dot inlays with white dots on sides
  • Graph Tech XL Black Tusq nut (45mm) with 2-way adjustable truss rod
  • Schecter USA M-50 and P-50 pickups
  • Darkglass Tone Capsule 18v EQ system with bass/mid/upper-mid contour
  • Schecter Diamond Custom bridge with string-thru or top-load capability
  • TonePros "M Series" tuners

This Reaper-5 Lefty combines professional-grade construction with responsive playability and characterful tone, making it an instrument worthy of serious consideration This navigating complex progressive arrangements or grooving through funk-heavy material.

item.title: Schecter Charles Berthoud CB-4 Lefty Bass See Thru Black Satin — $1,549
item.description:
$1,549 · Brand New · LV Guitars

Schecter Charles Berthoud CB-4 Lefty Bass – See Thru Black Satin

This Schecter Charles Berthoud CB-4 Lefty Bass delivers exceptional playability with its comfortable, modern body contours and responsive neck that makes navigating the fretboard feel effortless. The through-body construction and quality hardware produce a punchy, articulate tone with sustain that cuts through any mix, offering that perfect balance between defined attack and warm low-end resonance. The See Thru Black Satin finish showcases the beautiful wood grain beneath while providing a naturally worn-in aesthetic that improves with playing.

Playability & Feel

The walnut neck with Padauk stringers and dual carbon fiber reinforcement rods provides stability and responsiveness across the entire fretboard. With a thin "C" neck profile measuring 0.787" at the first fret and 0.866" at the 12th fret, combined with a 34" scale and 24 jumbo frets on a 16" radius rosewood fretboard, this bass rewards both precision work and fluid movement across positions.

Tone & Electronics

The lightweight ash body with flamed maple top paired with EMG HZ direct-mount pickups and active 3-band EQ delivers articulate, punchy response with excellent clarity and definition. A Graph Tech XL Black Tusq nut at 38mm width ensures clean string transmission and tonal integrity from nut to bridge.

Key Features
  • Left-handed configuration
  • Lightweight ash body with flamed maple top
  • See Thru Black Satin finish
  • Walnut neck with Padauk stringers and 2 carbon fiber reinforcement rods
  • Rosewood fretboard with 24 jumbo frets
  • 34" scale length
  • Thin "C" neck profile (0.787" – 0.866")
  • 16" fretboard radius
  • EMG HZ direct-mount pickups with EMG active 3-band EQ
  • Grover Bass 144 Series tuners
  • Graph Tech XL Black Tusq nut (38mm width)
  • Abalone offset dot inlays with side dot markers

This instrument rewards seasoned lefty bassists and players seeking a versatile bass with consistent tone, comfortable ergonomics, and the kind of playability that lets your musicianship shine through across funk, rock, jazz, and contemporary styles.

item.title: Schecter Simon Gallup Ultra Spitfire Bass White with Red Lion — $1,449
item.description:
$1,449 · Brand New · LV Guitars

Schecter Simon Gallup Ultra Spitfire Bass White with Red Lion

This Schecter Simon Gallup Ultra Spitfire Bass combines ergonomic comfort with professional responsiveness. The thin "C" neck shape and balanced body design facilitate both aggressive picked passages and expressive fingerstyle work, while the neck-thru construction provides solid sustain and resonance. The striking white finish with bold Red Lion graphic pairs form with function across every performance scenario.

Playability & Construction

The 34" scale with 22 medium frets and rosewood fretboard (16" radius) delivers consistent intonation and smooth playability across the entire range. Neck-thru mahogany construction with maple/walnut multi-ply provides both structural integrity and tonal resonance, while the thin "C" neck profile accommodates extended technique work without fatigue.

Tone & Electronics

EMG TBHZ pickups in bridge and neck positions deliver articulate, punchy response with clarity across the frequency spectrum. The Volume/Volume/Tone control layout offers immediate tonal shaping, while the Tune-O-Matic bridge with Stopbar tailpiece ensures stable tuning and consistent string-to-bridge contact for maximum sustain and definition.

Key Features
  • Gloss white finish with bold Red Lion graphic
  • Mahogany body with neck-thru maple/walnut multi-ply neck construction
  • Thin "C" neck shape for comfort and extended technique
  • 34" scale length with 22 medium frets
  • Rosewood fretboard with 16" radius and dot inlays
  • Fretboard width: 0.787" at 1st fret, 0.866" at 12th fret
  • EMG TBHZ pickups (bridge and neck positions)
  • Volume/Volume/Tone active control configuration
  • Tune-O-Matic bridge with Stopbar tailpiece
  • Grover tuning machines
  • Graph Tech XL Black Tusq nut, 38mm width
  • Grip Tip speed knobs included

This bass delivers professional-grade performance across demanding playing styles, backed by hardware and electronics that reward both precision and creative expression.

item.title: Schecter Model-T 4 String Exotic Black Limba Bass — $1,299
item.description:
$1,299 · Brand New · LV Guitars

Schecter Model-T 4 String Exotic Black Limba Bass

The Model-T delivers a buttery-smooth playability experience with its roasted maple neck and thin "C" profile that sits naturally in the hand across extended sessions. The exotic black limba body resonates with warm, articulate tone—punchy lows and singing mids that cut through a mix without sacrificing clarity. Built for both aggressive picking and nuanced fingerstyle work, this bass rewards technique with consistent sustain and responsiveness.

Playability & Feel

The roasted maple neck with ebony fretboard and thin "C" shape provides a fast, comfortable playing surface, while the 21 jumbo frets and 12" radius fretboard accommodate both modern lead work and traditional bass lines. A Graph Tech XL Black Tusq nut at 1.496" width ensures clean articulation and reliable string spacing for precise intonation.

Tone & Electronics

Paired Schecter MonsterTone pickups—a J-style bridge position and P-style neck position—deliver flexible tonal options controlled via dual volume and master tone knobs with metal knurled set-screw knobs. The Schecter Custom Bass Bridge with string-through design maximizes sustain and body resonance, while WSC KG400 tuners and a 2-way adjustable truss rod ensure stable tuning and neck adjustment.

Key Features
  • Body: Black Limba with flat top, natural satin finish, black 1-ply binding
  • Neck: Roasted Maple, thin "C" profile; 1st fret .787" / 12th fret .888" thickness
  • Fretboard: Ebony with 21 jumbo frets and abalone 3-piece block inlays
  • Scale: 34" (864mm) with 12" (305mm) fretboard radius
  • Nut: Graph Tech XL Black Tusq, 1.496" width
  • Pickups: Schecter MonsterTone-J (bridge) and MonsterTone-P (neck)
  • Controls: Volume/Volume/Tone with metal knurled set-screw knobs
  • Bridge: Schecter Custom Bass Bridge with string-through design
  • Tuners: WSC KG400
  • Truss Rod: 2-way adjustable with 5/32" (4mm) Allen nut
  • Hardware: Black finish throughout
  • Strings: Ernie Ball 4 String Super Long #2849 (.045–.105)

This Model-T combines premium tonewoods, thoughtful hardware, and dual-pickup versatility into an instrument that performs equally well anchoring a heavy rhythm section or locking into tight funk grooves.

item.title: Schecter Simon Gallup Corsair Electric Bass Red — $1,499
item.description:
$1,499 · Brand New · LV Guitars

Schecter Simon Gallup Corsair Electric Bass Red

The Corsair semi-hollowbody sits comfortably in your hands with naturally contoured body curves that make extended playing sessions effortless. This instrument delivers the aggressive sonic character that defined its namesake's work—punchy and articulate across the frequency spectrum, with pickups voiced to cut through dense arrangements without sacrificing warmth. The responsive neck and refined hardware combine to create a bass that feels equally at home in dark alternative soundscapes or high-definition studio work.

Playability & Feel

The thin "C"-shaped 3-piece maple neck measures 0.787" at the first fret and 0.866" at the 12th fret, providing a comfortable grip that sits naturally under your hand. A 34-inch scale length with 16-inch compound radius and 20 narrow jumbo frets offers excellent articulation and clarity, while the Graph Tech XL Black Tusq nut (38mm) ensures stable intonation and crisp note definition.

Tone & Electronics

Two EMG Thunderbird pickups paired with a 2-Band EMG Active E and Q system give you surgical control over your tone—Master Volume, Blend, and EQ adjustments let you shape everything from warm, woody lows to bright, snappy mids. The semi-hollowbody maple construction with cream multi-ply binding and arched top adds resonance and complexity that solid-body designs cannot match.

Key Features
  • Semi-hollowbody crafted from maple with cream multi-ply binding
  • Set-Neck construction with arched top
  • 3-piece maple neck with ebony fingerboard
  • 34-inch scale length
  • 16-inch compound radius with 20 narrow jumbo frets
  • Thin "C" shape neck (0.787" at 1st fret, 0.866" at 12th fret)
  • Pearloid block inlays
  • Graph Tech XL Black Tusq nut (38mm width)
  • Two EMG Thunderbird pickups
  • 2-Band EMG Active E and Q system with Master Volume and Blend
  • Tune-O-Matic bridge with Stopbar tailpiece
  • Schecter Light-Weight tuners with chrome hardware

The Corsair combines refined construction, active electronics, and proven hardware into a bass that rewards both aggressive playing and nuanced dynamic control.

item.title: ESP LTD Orion-4 Electric Bass Black Blast — $1,899
item.description:
$1,899 · Brand New · Studio Gears

ESP LTD Orion-4 Electric Bass — Black Blast

The Orion-4 delivers a balanced, comfortable playing experience built on a lightweight swamp ash body with a distinctive sandblasted texture. Its thin "U" profile neck and 34-inch scale make it equally approachable for extended sessions and quick studio work. This is a versatile workhorse designed for players who demand reliable tone and ergonomic design without compromise.

Playability & Construction

The 3-piece maple neck with bolt-on construction provides stability and sustain, while the Macassar ebony fretboard with 21 XJ frets ensures smooth, durable playability across the entire range. A 1.5-inch nut width and 15.75-inch fingerboard radius create a refined feel that accommodates both fingerstyle and pick-based techniques without fatigue.

Tone & Electronics

Twin Nordstrand Big Splits pickups deliver articulate, punchy response across the frequency spectrum, while the integrated stacked 2-band Nordstrand EQ with push/pull passive mode on the volume control provides tonal flexibility from vintage warmth to modern clarity. The Hipshot bridge ensures precise intonation and reliable hardware performance.

Key Features
  • Swamp ash body with sandblasted Black Blast textured finish
  • 3-piece maple neck with thin "U" profile
  • Bolt-on neck construction
  • 34-inch scale length
  • Macassar ebony fretboard with 21 XJ frets
  • 15.75-inch fingerboard radius
  • 1.5-inch nut width
  • Nordstrand Big Splits pickups (neck and bridge)
  • Stacked 2-band Nordstrand EQ with push/pull passive mode
  • Hipshot bridge
  • LTD vintage tuners
  • Includes hard case and D'Addario XL165 strings (.045/.065/.085/.105)

This Orion-4 combines Nordstrand electronics, premium hardware, and thoughtful ergonomics into a bass that handles studio sessions, live performance, and everyday playing with equal competence.

item.title: Schecter NP-4 String Nadja Peulen Bass Red Syren — $1,499
item.description:
$1,499 · Brand New · Studio Gears

Schecter NP-4 String Nadja Peulen Bass Red Syren

The Schecter NP-4 Nadja Peulen Signature Bass combines a comfortable, ergonomic body with a responsive neck that responds effortlessly to both tight, focused playing and exploratory melodic work. The Red Syren finish pairs with punchy, articulate electronics that cut through any mix with clarity, delivering warm lows and snappy mids that track your playing dynamics with precision. Built for players who demand versatility across metal, progressive rock, funk, and jazz fusion, this bass rewards both aggressive attack and subtle control in equal measure.

Playability & Construction

Neck-thru mahogany construction with carbon fiber reinforcement rods ensures stability and resonance across the instrument's 34-inch scale. The thin "C"-shaped neck measures 0.787" at the first fret and 0.905" at the twelfth fret, tapering smoothly to accommodate a wide range of hand sizes and playing techniques.

Tone & Electronics

EMG 35DC bridge pickup and 35P4 neck humbucker pickups deliver the articulate, punchy character this bass is known for, with a 2-band onboard EQ and dedicated blend control letting you shape tone from warm and rounded to bright and cutting. The Graph Tech XL Black Tusq nut and Schecter Diamond Custom bridge (supporting string-thru and top-load configurations) further refine sustain and tonal clarity.

Key Features
  • 4-string, right-handed configuration
  • Mahogany body with flat top and neck-thru construction
  • Three-piece mahogany neck with carbon fiber reinforcement rods
  • 34-inch scale length
  • Thin "C" neck shape: 0.787" (20mm) at first fret, 0.905" (23mm) at twelfth fret
  • Ebony fingerboard with 16-inch radius and 22 extra-jumbo frets
  • 38mm nut width with Graph Tech XL Black Tusq nut
  • Pearloid block inlays with Luminlay side dot markers
  • EMG 35DC bridge pickup and 35P4 neck humbucker
  • 2-band onboard EQ with blend and master volume controls
  • Schecter Diamond Custom bridge with string-thru and top-load support
  • Grover bass tuners with distressed chrome hardware finish

This signature model consolidates professional-grade construction, responsive electronics, and genuine playability into an instrument that inspires confidence across sessions and genres.

item.title: Schecter Stiletto Stealth-4 Pro EX Lefty Bass Satin Black — $1,649
item.description:
$1,649 · Brand New · Studio Gears

Schecter Stiletto Stealth-4 Pro EX Lefty Bass – Satin Black

This left-handed 4-string bass sits comfortably in your hands with its ergonomic contoured body and ultra-fast thin C-shape neck profile, making rapid position shifts and technical passages feel effortless across all playing styles. The aggressive active EMG electronics deliver a punchy, articulate tone with plenty of growl and presence that cuts through any mix, equally capable in funk, metal, progressive rock, and modern jazz contexts. Built on a 35-inch scale length with professional-grade construction, this Stiletto rewards both aggressive attack and nuanced dynamics.

Playability & Feel

The mahogany neck with maple stingers and ebony fretboard provide a fast, responsive playing surface, while the thin C-shape neck profile minimizes hand fatigue during extended sessions. 24 jumbo frets on a 16-inch radius give you comfortable access across the board, and the Graph Tech Black Tusq XL nut ensures reliable tuning stability and bright tone transmission. Dual-action truss rod reinforced with two carbon fiber rods keeps the neck stable under any string tension.

Tone & Electronics

The combination of EMG MMCS bridge pickup and EMG P neck pickup paired with EMG 2-band active EQ gives you surgical control over your tone, from tight percussive lows to singing midrange presence. Master volume, blend, and 2-band EQ controls let you dial in everything from tight, compressed funk grooves to dynamic lead lines without reaching for external gear.

Key Features
  • Left-handed configuration, 4-string with 35-inch scale length
  • Neck-thru body construction with ultra access and mahogany/maple build
  • Satin black finish
  • Ebony fretboard with 24 jumbo frets, 16-inch radius
  • Thin C-shape neck profile with dual carbon fiber rod reinforcement
  • Luminlay glow-in-the-dark side dot markers
  • EMG MMCS bridge pickup and EMG P neck pickup
  • EMG 2-band active EQ with master volume/blend controls
  • Graph Tech Black Tusq XL nut (38mm × 6mm)
  • Schecter Custom Bass String-Thru bridge
  • TonePros "M" Series tuners with 20:1 ratio
  • Dual-action truss rod

This Stiletto Stealth-4 Pro EX delivers the response, tone, and left-handed playability that makes you want to explore everything from tight grooves to expressive lead work.

item.title: ESP LTD MLB-4 Mike Leon Bass in Black Blast — $2,099
item.description:
$2,099 · Brand New · Studio Gears

ESP LTD MLB-4 Mike Leon Bass in Black Blast

The MLB-4 is a precision-crafted 4-string bass engineered for players who demand both comfortable ergonomics and articulate, modern tone. Its slim Extra Thin U neck contour and 35-inch scale length deliver effortless playability across extended ranges, while the swamp ash body and premium electronics package ensure clarity and responsiveness in any musical context.

Design & Build Quality

This bass features a bolt-on neck construction with a substantial 5-piece wenge/purpleheart neck for stability and tonal clarity. The Macassar ebony fingerboard accommodates 24 extra-jumbo stainless steel frets on a 400mm radius, providing both durability and smooth playability, while the 42mm nut width and Extra Thin U contour support precise finger positioning and reduced hand fatigue.

Tone & Electronics

Two Nordstrand Big Splits pickups deliver articulate, punchy tone with exceptional clarity across the frequency spectrum. The ABQ-3MS three-band EQ paired with individual volume controls for each pickup grant extensive sonic shaping, from focused fundamentals to aggressive presence—all powered by active electronics for consistent output and dynamic range.

Key Features
  • Swamp ash body with Black Blast finish
  • 5-piece wenge/purpleheart neck with bolt-on construction
  • Macassar ebony fingerboard
  • 400mm fingerboard radius
  • Extra Thin U neck contour
  • 24 extra-jumbo stainless steel frets
  • 35-inch scale length
  • 42mm nut width
  • Gotoh tuners and Gotoh 404BO-4 hardtail bridge
  • Two Nordstrand Big Splits pickups
  • Individual pickup volume controls with ABQ-3MS three-band EQ
  • Includes B Bass form-fit case and D'Addario XL165 strings

This MLB-4 combines professional-grade construction with versatile active electronics, making it a capable instrument across contemporary musical styles.

item.title: ESP LTD FL-4 Fred Leclercq Bass Black Red Burst Satin — $1,899
item.description:
$1,899 · Brand New · Studio Gears

ESP LTD FL-4 Fred Leclercq Bass Black Red Burst Satin

The FL-4 delivers a distinctly modern playing experience with its extra-thin U-contoured neck and lightweight ergonomic profile. Designed in collaboration with Fred Leclercq, this four-string bass balances aggressive aesthetics with refined playability, making it a serious instrument for session and touring musicians. The neck-through construction and premium tonewoods ensure stability and resonance across extended playing sessions.

Playability & Feel

The extra-thin U neck contour paired with a 400mm fingerboard radius and 24 XJ stainless steel frets creates a fast, responsive playing surface that rewards both precision and speed. The 40mm molded nut maintains proper string spacing while keeping the instrument comfortable for extended use.

Tone & Electronics

An EMG 35P bridge pickup delivers the aggressive, articulate output this bass demands, while the neck-through alder body and 5-piece maple/purpleheart neck provide a solid harmonic foundation. The Hipshot A-style bridge and Grover Mini tuners ensure reliable tuning stability and precise intonation adjustment.

Key Features
  • Neck-through construction for maximum sustain and body resonance
  • 34" scale length — standard four-string configuration
  • Alder body with black red burst satin finish
  • 5-piece maple/purpleheart neck with extra-thin U contour
  • Macassar ebony fingerboard with black binding and red offset dot inlays
  • 24 XJ stainless steel frets with 400mm radius
  • 40mm molded nut
  • EMG 35P bridge pickup
  • Hipshot A-style bridge
  • Grover Mini tuning machines
  • Black hardware throughout
  • Includes deluxe ESP hardshell case

This is a purpose-built instrument for players who demand professional-grade components, modern ergonomics, and the tonal character to cut through a mix.

item.title: Greco PB600 Electric Precision Bass 1977 - Sunburst — $835.17
item.description:
$835.17 · Very Good · Pat´s Guitars

Trade in your electric guitar? It’s easy with us. Just contact us with the model and pictures.

Specifications:

  • Country of manufacture: Japan
  • Neck dimensions: 1st fret 21mm / 12th fret 22.6mm
  • Nut width: 41.5mm
  • Pickups: Original - Maxon Bass Pickups 
  • Pots: Original 
  • Weight: 3.68kg

Condition:

  • Few signs of use, purely cosmetic and without impact on sound or playability – not all are visible in the pictures. 
  • Frets – Good condition: Frets with minimal signs of use, very playable.
  • The pickup height adjustment is not possible because the foam under the pickguard is too thin.

Setup: Each guitar receives a basic setup and is ready to play. The adjustment is made to the best of our knowledge and belief – individual modifications such as string action or intonation may be necessary depending on playing style and personal preferences.

Note: When shipping to countries outside the EU, additional taxes or fees (e.g., customs duties) may apply, which are not covered or charged by us. These must be paid directly to the relevant customs or tax authorities. Please check with the appropriate authorities for more information.

item.title: Schecter JD Deservio J-4 Electric Bass Gloss Black — $1,199
item.description:
$1,199 · Brand New · Studio Gears

Schecter JD DeServio J-4 Electric Bass – Gloss Black

The JD DeServio J-4 sits comfortably in the hands with its slim thin 'C' neck profile and balanced weight distribution, making extended playing sessions fatigue-free. This right-handed bass delivers articulate, punchy tone through dual Schecter USA MonsterTone-J pickups and rewards both precision work and aggressive playing styles. Built to professional standards with premium hardware and materials, it's a versatile workhorse for session players and touring musicians.

Playability & Comfort

The 34" scale with 22 X-Jumbo frets on a 16" radius ebony fretboard provides fast, responsive action and smooth string bending across the entire neck. The thin 'C' profile measures .787" at the 1st fret and .866" at the 12th fret, accommodating various hand sizes and playing techniques without compromising control or speed. Luminlay glow-in-the-dark side dot markers aid navigation in low-light performance settings.

Tone & Electronics

Dual Schecter USA MonsterTone-J pickups deliver punchy mids and articulate highs with solid low-end foundation, while the Volume/Volume/Tone control layout with push-pull coil-tap allows instant tonal shaping from thick and full to bright and focused. The Hipshot 'A' Style bass bridge with string-thru body or top-load options ensures rock-solid intonation and sustained resonance across all four strings.

Key Features
  • Body: Alder with gloss black finish
  • Bolt-on construction for sustained resonance and easy neck access
  • Neck: Maple with carbon fiber reinforcement rods
  • Fretboard: Ebony with 22 X-Jumbo frets and 16" radius
  • Scale: 34" (863mm) with 1.496" nut width
  • Thin 'C' neck profile (.787" at 1st fret, .866" at 12th fret)
  • Hardware: Black chrome finish with Hipshot 'A' style bridge
  • Pickups: Dual Schecter USA MonsterTone-J
  • Controls: Volume/Volume/Tone with push-pull coil-tap
  • Tuners: WSC KG400
  • Nut: Graph Tech XL Black Tusq
  • Side markers: Luminlay glow-in-the-dark

The JD DeServio J-4 combines premium construction, reliable electronics, and professional-grade hardware into a instrument built to handle studio and stage demands.
